title: Entity Component System
description: Annotation-driven ECS: @Component abstract classes are merged by code-gen into concrete entity classes + generated interfaces (c-suffix). @EntityDef triggers generation of a final/non-final concrete class. @BaseComponent marks the root base. @Import pulls fields from another merged component.

classes.name: EntityComp
classes.notes: Root base component for all entities. self() is replaced with 'this' post-codegen. No @EntityDef — not directly instantiated.

classes.name: BuildingComp
key_methods: Building init(Tile tile, Team team, boolean shouldAdd, int rotation)
classes.notes: Base building entity; genInterface=false means Buildingc is NOT auto-generated from this comp, it's defined separately. isFinal=false means the generated Building class is subclassable. Modules (Power/Item/Liquid) are optional compositions. Proximity stores adjacent buildings.

classes.name: UnitComp
fields: @Import float x, y, rotation, maxHealth, drag, armor, hitSize, health, shield, ammo, dragMultiplier, armorOverride, speedMultiplier
transient float shadowAlpha, healTime, drownTime, splashTimer
classes.notes: Core unit logic. @Replace overrides component-default implementations. controller field is private — accessed via controller()/controller(UnitController). type drives all stats. Abilities updated in update() loop. Death sequence: kill() → unitDeath RPC → killed() → destroy() → remove().

classes.name: PlayerComp
key_methods: void sendMessage(String text, Player from, String unformatted)
classes.notes: Player IS a UnitController — it wraps a Unit entity. unit() field is @ReadOnly (set via unit(Unit) method only). Death detected by unit==null or !unit.isValid(). afterSync() handles anti-rubberbanding for unit transitions. Spawning delegated to CoreBuild.requestSpawn().

generated_interfaces.name: Entityc
generated_interfaces.notes: Auto-generated interface exposing EntityComp public API. All entity types implement this.

generated_interfaces.name: Buildingc
generated_interfaces.notes: Manually defined or generated elsewhere. BuildingComp implements it as a marker. @EntityDef uses it as the entity interface type.

generated_interfaces.name: Unitc
generated_interfaces.notes: Generated interface for UnitComp. Unit concrete class implements Unitc.

generated_interfaces.name: Playerc
generated_interfaces.notes: Generated from @EntityDef on PlayerComp. Player concrete class is the generated impl.

key_relationships: EntityComp <<BaseComponent>> — base of all comp hierarchy (annotated @BaseComponent)
BuildingComp @Component(base=true) — merged with EntityComp fields at codegen; implements Buildingc
UnitComp @Component(base=true) — merged with EntityComp; implements Unitc
PlayerComp @Component(base=true) — merged with EntityComp; implements UnitController + Entityc
PlayerComp implements UnitController — Player directly controls a Unit
UnitComp.type: UnitType — composition (drives all unit stats/behavior)
BuildingComp.block: Block — type reference (analogous to UnitType for units)
BuildingComp.proximity: Seq<Building> — adjacency list of neighboring buildings
PlayerComp.unit: Unit [0..1] — player references the unit it controls
PlayerComp.con: NetConnection [0..1] — null for local player

ecs_annotation_summary.@Component: Marks an abstract class as an ECS component. Multiple components are merged by annotation processor into a single concrete class.
ecs_annotation_summary.@Component(base=true): Marks a component as the primary base — merged entity class named after @EntityDef target.
ecs_annotation_summary.@BaseComponent: On EntityComp: marks the universal base injected into every entity.
ecs_annotation_summary.@EntityDef(value={Xc.class}): On a component: triggers generation of a concrete entity class that implements the listed interface(s).
ecs_annotation_summary.@Import fieldName: Field is provided by another component; skipped in this component's generated code, taken from the real owner.
ecs_annotation_summary.@SyncLocal: Field is synced over network but only written by the local client.
ecs_annotation_summary.@ReadOnly: Field only writable internally; getter exposed in interface but no setter.
ecs_annotation_summary.@Replace: Method overrides the default implementation from another component's interface.
ecs_annotation_summary.@InternalImpl: Method is implemented internally by the codegen system.

important_notes: All concrete entity classes (Building, Unit, Player) are code-generated — they do NOT exist as source files; they exist in mindustry.gen at compile time.
UnitComp has the most complex lifecycle: update() → controller.updateUnit() → kill() → Call.unitDeath(id) RPC → killed() → destroy() → remove().
PlayerComp.update(): if alive → set(unit) position sync; if dead → deathTimer → bestCore().requestSpawn(self()).
BuildingComp.init() vs create(): create() sets up block/team/modules; init() positions on tile and optionally adds to world.
The @Import annotation is crucial: UnitComp declares @Import x, y, team etc. — those fields are actually owned by PosComp/TeamComp/HealthComp respectively and merged in.
PlayerComp implements UnitController making Player a first-class controller; isLogicControllable() returns false (players cannot be logic-controlled).
UnitComp.afterSync() calls setType(this.type) to reinitialize stats from UnitType after network sync.
